name: Clarified Butter- Master Chefs
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the unsalted butter into pieces.
2. Melt the butter slowly in a saucepan.
3. Skim off as much foam as possible from the surface of the melted butter.
4. Carefully pour off all the clear, golden liquid (this is the clarified butter), leaving all milky residue in the bottom of the pan. Discard the residue.
5. Clarified butter can be stored well, tightly sealed, in the refrigerator or freezer. One half pound of butter (2 sticks) yields about 3/4 cup clarified butter.
Source: New York's Master Chefs, Bon Appetit Magazine
Written by Richard Sax, Photographs by Nancy McFarland
The Knapp Press, Los Angeles, 1985
Chef: Andree Abramoff, Andree's Mediterranean Restaurant, New York
